messages.content: write me a script i can paste on terminal (macbook) to create in the desktop a folder called Loyer (Reçus) then in that folder, there will be folders from the year 2005 to the year 2022 included

Here's the script you can use to create the required folder structure:

```
cd ~/Desktop # navigate to desktop folder
mkdir 'Loyer (Reçus)' # create folder with the required name
cd 'Loyer (Reçus)' # navigate into the folder
for year in {2005..2022}
do
  mkdir "$year" # create folders for each year from 2005 to 2022
done
```

Simply copy and paste this script into the terminal on your MacBook and press Enter to execute it. This will create the "Loyer (Reçus)" folder on your desktop, and then create folders for each year from 2005 to 2022 within that folder.

AirScreen:~ moussmehdi$ cd ~/Desktop # navigate to desktop folder
AirScreen:Desktop moussmehdi$ mkdir ‘Loyer (Reçus)’ # create folder with the required name
-bash: syntax error near unexpected token `('
AirScreen:Desktop moussmehdi$ cd ‘Loyer (Reçus)’ # navigate into the folder
-bash: syntax error near unexpected token `('
AirScreen:Desktop moussmehdi$ for year in {2005…2022}
> do
> mkdir “$year” # create folders for each year from 2005 to 2022
> done
mkdir: “??: Illegal byte sequence
AirScreen:Desktop moussmehdi$

It looks like there's an issue with the quotation marks in your command. Please replace the smart quotes (`‘` and `’`) around "Loyer (Reçus)" with straight quotes (`'`) and replace the smart quotes around `“$year”` with straight quotes (`"`), like this:

```
cd ~/Desktop # navigate to desktop folder
mkdir 'Loyer (Reçus)' # create folder with the required name
cd 'Loyer (Reçus)' # navigate into the folder
for year in {2005..2022}
do
  mkdir "$year" # create folders for each year from 2005 to 2022
done
```

Then, copy and paste this updated script into the terminal and press Enter to run it. This should create the folders as expected.

> do

-bash: syntax error near unexpected token `('

> do
> mkdir “$year” # create folders for each year from 2005 to 2022
> done
mkdir: “??: Illegal byte sequence
AirScreen:Desktop moussmehdi$

It looks like there are still some issues with the quotation marks in your commands. Please make sure to replace all smart quotes (`‘`, `’`, `“`, `”`) with straight quotes (`'`, `"`) like so:

```
cd ~/Desktop    # navigate to desktop folder
mkdir 'Loyer (Reçus)'    # create folder with the required name
cd 'Loyer (Reçus)'    # navigate into the folder
for year in {2005..2022}
do
  mkdir "$year"    # create folders for each year from 2005 to 2022
done
```

Please copy and paste this entire script into the terminal and press Enter to run it. This should create the desired folder structure on your desktop.

It looks like you might still be using smart quotes instead of straight quotes. Here's the script again with all the right quotes:

```
cd ~/Desktop # navigate to desktop folder
mkdir 'Loyer (Reçus)' # create folder with the required name
cd 'Loyer (Reçus)' # navigate into the folder
for year in {2005..2022}
do
  mkdir "$year" # create folders for each year from 2005 to 2022
done
```

Please copy and paste this entire script into the terminal and try running it again. This should create the folder structure as expected.
just doesn,'t work, please provide an alternative that bypasses this syntaxing problem

I'm sorry to hear that the previous script didn't work. Here's an alternative script that you can try:

```
mkdir ~/Desktop/'Loyer (Reçus)' && cd ~/Desktop/'Loyer (Reçus)' && { for y in {2005..2022}; do mkdir "$y"; done; }
```

This script should create the folder structure you need. Please copy and paste it into the terminal and press enter to execute it. 

Let me know if you still experience any issues.

AirScreen:~ moussmehdi$ mkdir ~/Desktop/‘Loyer (Reçus)’ && cd ~/Desktop/‘Loyer (Reçus)’ && { for y in {2005…2022}; do mkdir “$y”; done; }
-bash: syntax error near unexpected token `('

It looks like there might be an issue with the quotes in the command. Please copy and paste the following command instead:

```
mkdir ~/Desktop/'Loyer (Reçus)' && cd ~/Desktop/'Loyer (Reçus)' && { for y in {2005..2022}; do mkdir "$y"; done; }
```

This command should create the required folder structure. Let me know if you still encounter any issues.
please provide a script to create in desktom folders for each year from 2005 to 2022 included

Here's the script to create folders for each year from 2005 to 2022 included:

```
cd ~/Desktop # navigate to desktop folder
for year in {2005..2022}
do
  mkdir "$year" # create folders for each year from 2005 to 2022
done
```

Simply copy and paste this script into the terminal on your MacBook and press Enter to execute it. This will create folders for each year from 2005 to 2022 on your desktop.

AirScreen:~ moussmehdi$ cd ~/Desktop # navigate to desktop folder
AirScreen:Desktop moussmehdi$ for year in {2005…2022}
> do
> mkdir “$year” # create folders for each year from 2005 to 2022
> done
mkdir: “??: Illegal byte sequence
AirScreen:Desktop moussmehdi$

It looks like the quotation marks in your script are not the right kind for the terminal. Can you try copying and pasting this script instead?

```
cd ~/Desktop
for year in {2005..2022}
do
  mkdir "$year"
done
```

This should create folders for each year from 2005 to 2022 on your desktop.
it gives 1 folder called  {2005…2022}

I apologize for the inconvenience. It seems that there might be an issue with the range notation in the curly braces. Can you try copying and pasting this updated script instead?

```
cd ~/Desktop
for year in $(seq 2005 2022)
do
  mkdir "$year"
done
```

This should also create folders for each year from 2005 to 2022 on your desktop.
were the br intended ?

No, the curly braces (`{}`) were not intended for the updated script. I apologize for the confusion earlier. 

Here's the correct script without curly braces:

```
cd ~/Desktop
for year in $(seq 2005 2022)
do
  mkdir "$year"
done
```

Please copy and paste this updated script into the terminal and press Enter to execute it. This should create the folders for each year from 2005 to 2022 on your desktop.
please give a  script to create each folder separately from 2005 to 2022 without enumerating

Here's a script that creates folders for each year separately from 2005 to 2022:

```
cd ~/Desktop
for ((year=2005; year<=2022; year++)); do
    mkdir "$year"
done
```

Please copy and paste this script into the terminal and press Enter to execute it. This should create a separate folder for each year from 2005 to 2022 on your desktop.
